name: Paula White
role: Senior Pastor, City of Destiny Church, Apopka, Florida; Former Special Adviser to President Trump
incident: Paula White, Trump's personal pastor and special adviser to the White House Faith and Opportunity Initiative, delivered a prayer on November 5, 2020 calling for angels from Africa and South America to come to the United States and overturn the election results. White had previously preached extensively about respecting democratic institutions and the rule of law as Christian values.

name: Greg Locke
role: Lead Pastor, Global Vision Bible Church, Mount Juliet, Tennessee
incident: Greg Locke, who had preached extensively that divorce is sinful and publicly condemned other pastors for remarrying after divorce, divorced his wife of 21 years and married his church secretary within the same year. Locke had previously called out fellow pastors on social media for moral failures related to marriage and divorce, writing publicly that leaders must be held to a higher standard.

name: Carl Lentz
role: Former Lead Pastor, Hillsong Church New York
incident: Carl Lentz, lead pastor of Hillsong New York who built a celebrity congregation including Justin Bieber and Kevin Durant while preaching extensively about sexual integrity and moral leadership, was fired by Hillsong founder Brian Houston on November 4, 2020 for moral failures. Lentz subsequently admitted to infidelity. A former housekeeper later filed a lawsuit alleging Lentz had sexually coerced her beginning in 2016.

name: Brian Houston
role: Founder and Global Senior Pastor, Hillsong Church
incident: Brian Houston, founder of Hillsong Church, resigned on March 21, 2022 after an independent investigation found he had behaved inappropriately toward two women at separate church conferences over a decade. Houston had built Hillsong into a global brand on messages of moral integrity and family values. The same investigation also revealed Houston had failed to report his father's child sexual abuse to authorities.

name: Creflo Dollar
role: Senior Pastor, World Changers Church International, College Park, Georgia
incident: Creflo Dollar, prosperity gospel televangelist and founder of World Changers Church International, launched a fundraising campaign in March 2015 asking his congregation and online followers to donate $65 million toward a Gulfstream G650 private jet. Dollar argued the jet was needed for global ministry. Dollar was already operating two Gulfstream jets at the time of the fundraising request and was simultaneously teaching that financial giving to his ministry was the path to personal financial blessing.

name: Mark Driscoll
role: Founder, Mars Hill Church, Seattle, Washington; Founder, Trinity Church, Scottsdale, Arizona
incident: Mark Driscoll, who built Mars Hill Church in Seattle to 15,000 members while preaching male authority, spiritual submission, and accountability, was forced to resign from Mars Hill in 2014 after investigations documented years of bullying, plagiarism, and misuse of church funds. Driscoll subsequently founded Trinity Church in Scottsdale, where former members and staff documented the same pattern of behavior beginning in 2021, including spiritual abuse, financial misconduct, and firing staff who raised concerns.

name: Perry Noble
role: Founder and Former Senior Pastor, NewSpring Church, Anderson, South Carolina
incident: Perry Noble, founder of NewSpring Church which grew to 32,000 members under his leadership, was fired by the church's board of directors in July 2016 for alcohol abuse and what the board described as the related effects on his marriage. Noble had preached extensively about personal discipline, accountability, and the destructive effects of addiction from his pulpit.

name: James MacDonald
role: Founder and Former Senior Pastor, Harvest Bible Chapel, Rolling Meadows, Illinois
incident: James MacDonald, founder of Harvest Bible Chapel which grew to 13,000 members, was fired by the church's elders on February 12, 2019 after recordings surfaced in which he made vulgar statements about colleagues and a journalist investigating him. Subsequent investigations documented that MacDonald had pressured church staff to make fraudulent insurance claims, improperly used church funds for personal expenses including a hunting ranch, and led a pattern of bullying and spiritual abuse spanning years.

name: Eddie Long
role: Senior Pastor, New Birth Missionary Baptist Church, Lithonia, Georgia
incident: Bishop Eddie Long, senior pastor of New Birth Missionary Baptist Church with a congregation of 25,000 and a prominent voice against same-sex marriage, faced lawsuits filed on September 22, 2010 by four young men alleging he had used spiritual authority and gifts to coerce them into sexual relationships beginning when they were teenagers. Long had been a leading figure in organizing a march against same-sex marriage in Atlanta in 2004.

name: Ted Haggard
role: Former President, National Association of Evangelicals; Founder, New Life Church, Colorado Springs, Colorado
incident: Ted Haggard, president of the National Association of Evangelicals representing 30 million American Christians and one of the most prominent anti-gay-marriage voices in the country, resigned on November 2, 2006 after a male escort named Mike Jones disclosed that Haggard had been his client for three years and had purchased methamphetamine from him. Haggard had met weekly with President George W. Bush and regularly advised the White House on faith policy.

name: Tony Spell
role: Lead Pastor, Life Tabernacle Church, Central, Louisiana
incident: Tony Spell, lead pastor of Life Tabernacle Church, held in-person church services of over 1,000 people on March 31, 2020 in direct violation of Louisiana Governor John Bel Edwards' emergency stay-at-home order. Spell was charged with six counts of unlawful assembly. Spell argued that the government had no authority over the church, then subsequently requested and accepted a Paycheck Protection Program loan from the federal government whose authority he had publicly rejected.

name: Rodney Howard-Browne
role: Founder and Senior Pastor, The River at Tampa Bay Church, Tampa, Florida
incident: Rodney Howard-Browne, South African-born founder of The River at Tampa Bay Church, was arrested on March 30, 2020 on charges of unlawful assembly and violation of a public health emergency order after holding two packed church services on March 29 in defiance of Hillsborough County's stay-at-home order. Howard-Browne had built his ministry on teaching that divine healing supersedes medical authority, while simultaneously requiring all church staff to sign legal liability waivers.
